column20:
Well said Borja, thanks for sharing. We'll be in touch shortly to share the article once published!



Are you headed to Eth Denver?
column21:
The line between real value and hype is clear. Hype is built on narrative, big promises, and artificial traction; it works as long as attention and capital keep flowing. Real value shows up when technology removes concrete friction, cuts costs, or creates measurable efficiency in the real world.

Projects that last don’t need to explain their technology — they get adopted because they work. They are economically sustainable, aligned with real operational constraints, and keep delivering even when the market stops applauding. Mass adoption doesn’t happen when people understand the innovation, but when they can no longer operate without it.
